summary: Full backend + SPA testing of the Auditor Workbench iteration adding evidence artifacts + per-phase article-level citations. 11/11 backend pytest cases pass against the preview URL (6 artifacts_and_citations + 5 new_backend_endpoints covering auth for all 4 roles, bad-secret 401, runs list, certificates list, and full revoke flow with GET verification). SPA fully exercised: login redirect, 4 role cards, dashboard (4 health cards + Recent Audit Runs + Recent Certificates populated from API), 8-item sidebar nav with hotkeys, New Audit wizard with 9 steps and 18 pre-filled evidence artifacts, one-click 'Run full demo audit' completing all 9 phases with green checks in ~15s, final Monitoring panel showing EU-AI-ACT Art.72 -> Monitoring Dashboard (present) and ISO-42001 Clause 9.1 missing incident_report, run detail (#/runs/:id) with 4 KPIs (Phases 9/9, Artifacts 18, Citations 23, Missing 1), 9-phase timeline with hash chain + ARTICLES EVIDENCED — ARTIFACTS INSPECTED (Fairness cites EU-AI-ACT Art.10 -> Bias Test Output -> pass and NIST-AI-RMF MEASURE 2.2 -> Fairness Metrics Report -> pass), Certificates list with detail page (schemaValid/signatureValid/notExpired/notRevoked = PASS + credentialSubject JSON + Revoke button), Verify Certificate flow returns verified=PASS, Event Ledger 'Recent Events (delivered)' table with Event/Phase/Run/Status/Attempts/At columns, Reaudit form, and MCP/API page with curl examples and all 11 governance tools (intake_register, map_regulatory_scope, classify_risk, check_data_protection, evaluate_fairness, test_robustness, verify_explainability, assemble_certification, configure_monitoring, trigger_reaudit, get_audit_run). Signout clears sessionStorage and redirects to #/login; protected routes redirect to #/login when logged out.

ui_bugs.issue: Guest access to #/verify is blocked — when sessionStorage has no token, navigating to /#/verify redirects to /#/login. Spec requires /verify to be a public/guest-only route because /api/v1/certificates/{id}/verify is a public endpoint. Sign-out flow currently prevents an unauthenticated user from validating a certificate URN they received externally.

action_items: Fix route guard in /app/mcp-server/ui/workbench.js so that #/verify is accessible without a session token (public/guest route). All other routes correctly redirect to #/login as expected.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all core flows work; one route-guard bug on /verify guest access)

seed_data_creation: Demo audit run 8880f2b1-eb34-4a29-be04-4ff335b6b42e created via wizard (demo-cvscreener-mrt19cvl). One TEST_ revoke performed on an active certificate during test_certificate_revoke_flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SPA at /, hash routing (#/login, #/dashboard, #/audit/new, #/runs/:id, #/certificates, #/certificates/:id, #/verify, #/events, #/reaudit, #/mcp). Wizard 'Run full demo audit' button seeds 18 artifacts and runs 9 phases sequentially in ~12-15s. Run detail hash-chain visible per phase; ARTICLES EVIDENCED — ARTIFACTS INSPECTED section renders per-phase citations. New pytest file at /app/tests/governance/test_new_backend_endpoints.py exercises auth/runs/certificates/revoke.
rca of the issue: Guest /verify redirect: the SPA's hash-router guard in /app/mcp-server/ui/workbench.js appears to require a session token for every route except #/login. The /api/v1/certificates/{id}/verify endpoint is public (no auth header needed), so the UI page should also be reachable without a session. Fix: whitelist '#/verify' (and possibly '#/login') in the route guard so unauthenticated users see the Verify Certificate form.
